response.justification: The role's most critical minimum qualification is 8 years of C++ experience, and the resume reveals only marginal C++ exposure — roughly 6 months at National Instruments (2014–2015) and a bachelor thesis side project — while the candidate's actual expertise spans TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. Beyond the language mismatch, there is no evidence of formal technical leadership over a cloud compute platform, no cloud architecture ownership at scale, and the candidate has been on a career gap since October 2025. The distributed systems work at Dunnhumby is the most relevant experience, but it falls well short of the depth and leadership scope the role demands.
strengths: Hands-on distributed systems exposure handling millions of HTTP requests per second at Dunnhumby, demonstrating real high-throughput infrastructure work
Broad DevOps and infrastructure familiarity including Kubernetes, Docker, AWS CDK, CI/CD pipelines, and self-hosted tooling across multiple roles
Consistent knowledge-sharing culture evidenced by Staff-level positions, public workshops, conference talks, and open-source library authorship
concerns: C++ experience is negligible (~6 months total) versus the 8-year minimum requirement — the primary languages throughout the career are TypeScript, JavaScript, Node.js, and DLang
No formal technical leadership role over cloud or infrastructure engineering teams; Staff titles were individual-contributor oriented rather than people or project leadership
No demonstrated cloud platform architecture ownership or experience balancing short- and long-term platform strategy at enterprise scale
